issues.title: Missing @source directive for packages/app-components
issues.description: All 7 apps scan 'packages/ui/src' but none scan 'packages/app-components/src'. The AppNavigation component (packages/app-components/src/components/AppNavigation.tsx) uses Tailwind utilities 'backface-hidden' and 'contain-[paint]' that are not present in any app's own source files. Since Tailwind v4 uses JIT compilation and only generates CSS for scanned classes, these utilities will not be generated and will silently produce no CSS in any app. Every app's globals.css needs: @source "../../../../packages/app-components/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}";
issues.fix:
Add the following @source directive to all 7 apps' globals.css files (apps/admin, apps/auth, apps/landing, apps/payments, apps/playground, apps/store, apps/studio):
@source "../../../../packages/app-components/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}";

issues.title: Tailwind class strings extracted to JS constants
issues.description: Two Tailwind class strings are extracted to module-level constants: MUTED_STYLE = "bg-muted text-muted-foreground" and WARNING_STYLE = "bg-warning/10 text-warning". Per project rules (code-review-standards.md, dry-principle.md), Tailwind utility classes should never be extracted to JS/TS constants — they are designed to be inlined in JSX. The correct approach when a class combination is reused is to create a component or apply the classes directly in the STATUS_COLOR_MAP object.
issues.fix:
Remove MUTED_STYLE and WARNING_STYLE constants and inline the class strings directly in STATUS_COLOR_MAP:
const STATUS_COLOR_MAP: Partial<Record<OrderStatus, string>> = {
  approved: "bg-success/10 text-success",
  rejected: "bg-destructive/10 text-destructive",
  expired: "bg-muted text-muted-foreground",
  pending_verification: "bg-warning/10 text-warning",
  evidence_requested: "bg-warning/10 text-warning",
  awaiting_payment: "bg-info/10 text-info",
  pending: "bg-muted text-muted-foreground",
};
And update the fallback in line 27: ?? "bg-muted text-muted-foreground"

issues.title: Low-opacity text may fall below WCAG contrast threshold
issues.description: Three instances of 'text-muted-foreground/40' are used for decorative em-dash placeholders (—) and 'No receipt' text in table cells (lines 101, 118, 132). The muted-foreground color is oklch(0.44 0 0) in light mode. At 40% opacity over white background, the computed contrast ratio is approximately 2.6:1, which is below the WCAG 2.1 AA minimum of 3:1 for UI components and 4.5:1 for text. In dark mode, muted-foreground is oklch(0.57 0 0) at 40% opacity over dark background, also likely below 3:1. While these are decorative/non-critical indicators, the 'No receipt' text in line 132 is meaningful content.
issues.fix: For decorative em-dash (—) placeholders, consider 'text-muted-foreground/60' which should maintain sufficient contrast. For the 'No receipt' text in line 132, use full 'text-muted-foreground' without opacity reduction. Or keep at /40 with a comment noting this is intentionally decorative.

issues.title: AppNavigation uses performance-optimized CSS utilities correctly
issues.description: The nav element uses 'will-change-transform', 'backface-hidden', 'transform-gpu', and 'contain-[paint]' — these are valid Tailwind v4 performance optimization utilities for sticky navigation. The pattern is correct. This note is filed because these classes depend on STYLE-001 being fixed (the @source directive for app-components) to be properly generated by Tailwind JIT.
issues.fix: No change needed here. Fix STYLE-001 to ensure these classes are scanned and generated.

issues.title: All apps have identical globals.css — CSS consistency is maintained
issues.description: All 7 app globals.css files are byte-for-byte identical: they all import tailwindcss, tw-animate-css, ui/globals, and have @source directives for local src and packages/ui. The shared theme (colors, typography, semantic utilities) is centralized in packages/ui/src/styles/ and consistently imported via 'ui/globals'. No CSS variable drift was found between apps. The @theme inline block and :root/.dark variable definitions are shared from a single source of truth.
issues.fix: No action needed. This is a positive finding documenting correct implementation.

issues.title: Status badge uses semantic color tokens correctly
issues.description: The STATUS_COLOR_MAP uses proper semantic tokens throughout: bg-success/10, text-success, bg-destructive/10, text-destructive, bg-muted, text-muted-foreground, bg-warning/10, text-warning, bg-info/10, text-info. No raw Tailwind palette colors (red-*, green-*, blue-*, etc.) are used. This is the correct pattern.
